title: HealthEquity gains as BMO upgrades on long-term growth outlook
content:
[The New York Stock Exchange on the Wall street sign]
Dmitry Vinogradov

Shares of HealthEquity (HQY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HQY]) traded higher on Thursday after BMO Capital Markets boosted its longer-term growth estimates for the company and upgraded the stock to Outperform from Market Perform.

Citing “overblown” AI-related fears and BMO’s revised longer-range growth estimates for HQY, analyst Sean Dodge argued that the Draper, Utah-based health savings accounts administrator trades at an attractive valuation, providing investors with a compelling entry point.

Dodge lifted his longer-term growth estimates for HealthEquity (HQY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HQY]) to high single digits/low double digits compared to mid-single digits/high single digits previously, citing multiple reasons, including the recent launch of the company’s Marketplace business.

“The Marketplace serves as another opportunity to monetize HQY's member base by simply acting as a referral partner into the programs and then facilitating incremental interchange revenue on the associated spend,” the analyst wrote. Dodge raised his price target on HQY to $105 from $85 per share.

title: Quant check on insurer stocks as Medicare Advantage rate boost drives gains
content:
[Medicare Advantage]
All_About_Najmi/iStock via Getty Images

As the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services announced that the average Medicare Advantage payments will be boosted by 2.48% in 2027 [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4572839-insurance-stocks-jump-on-medicare-advantage-rate-boost-humana-unitedhealth-lead-gains], major healthcare insurers sharply moved higher in after-hours trading on Monday.

The CMS payment rate is closely watched by the market and dictates how much insurers can charge for monthly premiums and benefits, directly influencing their profitability. Previously in January, the administration had proposed a much smaller 0.09% hike, which had pressured insurers’ stocks.

With a shift in sentiment translating to stock levels, Quant ratings provide a snapshot of which insurance provider names are screening strongest.

Here is the list:

 	*
HealthEquity (HQY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HQY]), Quant Rating: 3.57 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HQY/ratings/quant-ratings] 

 	*
Centene Corporation (CNC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CNC]), Quant Rating: 3.38 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CNC/ratings/quant-ratings] (up 4.3% after-hours on Monday)

 	*
Alignment Healthcare (ALHC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ALHC]), Quant Rating: 3.30 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ALHC/ratings/quant-ratings](up 12.5% after-hours on Monday)

 	*
UnitedHealth Group (UNH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/UNH]) , Quant Rating: 3.32 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/UNH/ratings/quant-ratings] (up 7.8% after-hours on Monday)

 	*
Progyny (PGNY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PGNY]), Quant Rating: 3.08 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PGNY/ratings/quant-ratings] (up 1.1% after-hours on Monday)

 	*
Elevance Health (ELV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ELV]), Quant Rating: 2.97 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ELV/ratings/quant-ratings] (up 6.7% after-hours on Monday)

 	*
Humana (HUM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HUM]), Quant Rating: 2.78 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HUM/ratings/quant-ratings] (up 11% after-hours on Monday)

 	*
Molina Healthcare (MOH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MOH]), Quant Rating: 2.61 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MOH/ratings/quant-ratings] (up 3.8% after-hours on Monday)

ETFs to track: (IXJ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IXJ]), (VHT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VHT]), (XBI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XBI]), (XLV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XLV]), (RSPH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RSPH]), (KIE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KIE]), (IAK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IAK]).
